summary: Audience tape with solid clarity (though bass‑heavy) captures a stellar 1974 show, highlighted by an iconic China Cat Sunflower → The Rider.
review: The recording is an audience source that delivers surprisingly clear detail for the era—Jerry’s lilt shines and Phil’s low end is booming, albeit a bit overpowering on some speakers. Musically the concert is praised as a “show‑stopper,” with the China Cat Sunflower → The Rider segue singled out as possibly the best version from ’73‑’74. Overall the performance earns five‑star enthusiasm despite minor tonal quirks.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviewers rate the show 5 stars and describe the recording as very good for an audience tape, yielding a high combined score; limited to one detailed review, confidence is moderate.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear enough for an audience tape with proper pitch; booming bass can overwhelm some systems and Jerry’s guitar distorts at high peaks, but overall very listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: High‑energy 1974 set with classic transitions; the China>Rider sequence is highlighted as a peak moment.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Lilt in his guitar is evident; distortion appears when he pushes the high end.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass is booming and can be overpowering on some speakers.
